question.text: A loan of Rs. 10,00,000 at 7% p.a. (monthly rest) for 10 years has an EMI of Rs. 11,610. In the first EMI, the principal repaid is Rs. 5,777. The interest component of the first EMI is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Interest for first month = Outstanding Principal × Monthly Rate = 10,00,000 × (7%/12) = 10,00,000 × 0.00583 = Rs. 5,833. Principal = EMI - Interest = 11,610 - 5,833 = Rs. 5,777 (confirms the given principal amount). **The Trap:** Rs. 7,000 is annual interest divided by 12 (10,00,000 × 7% / 12 = 5,833 ≈ 7,000/12 is wrong reasoning). **Cross-Exam Context:** PPMT and IPMT calculations are specifically tested in X-A (Ch 4). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding the interest-principal split helps clients see why prepayment in early years has maximum impact — most of the initial EMIs are just interest.
